rationale: All three lenses (blind, edge-case, acceptance) ran successfully. All five acceptance criteria pass. The acceptance and blind lenses raised a bare-await concern — that tests would fail because authenticate() rejects on 50 ms timeout — but reading the implementation confirms that authenticate() internally catches all rejections (including timeouts) in a try/catch block and resolves with a result object ({ success: false, error: '...' }); the concern is a false positive and the tests are structurally correct. Edge-case and blind lenses identified an unhandled EventEmitter 'error' event on the explorer.exe spawn (potential crash on ENOENT); deferred because the spec explicitly designates browser launch as best-effort with no error handling, explorer.exe is always present on the target corporate Windows platform, and the URL is unconditionally printed as a manual fallback. URL scheme validation concern is pre-existing (existed with the original open() call) and not introduced by this diff. 3 deferred findings, 0 blocking findings.

business_review.item: SSO URL is printed in the terminal before every browser launch attempt
business_review.notes: sso.auth.ts:112 — chalk.cyan console.log of the fallback URL appears unconditionally before the win32/else branch

business_review.item: On Windows, explorer.exe is spawned (no powershell.exe involved)
business_review.notes: sso.auth.ts:114-121 — win32 branch uses spawn('explorer.exe', [ssoUrl], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }).unref(); no PowerShell reference anywhere in the diff

business_review.item: On macOS/Linux, open() is called as before
business_review.notes: sso.auth.ts:122-123 — else branch calls await open(ssoUrl) unchanged from the original implementation

business_review.item: Three new unit tests pass (URL print, Windows path, macOS/Linux path)
business_review.notes: Three tests added in sso.auth.test.ts. Both the acceptance and blind lenses flagged a bare-await concern, but authenticate() handles the 50 ms timeout internally (try/catch returns { success: false, error: '...' } rather than throwing). Tests are correct and assertions will execute after authenticate() resolves. vi.mock('child_process') with importOriginal correctly intercepts the dynamic import inside authenticate().

business_review.item: Existing sso.auth.test.ts tests continue to pass
business_review.notes: deriveExpiresAt describe block is unmodified. New vi.mock declarations for open and child_process are additive and do not affect the pure-function deriveExpiresAt tests.

business_review.item: URL Print — two console.log calls in prescribed order before the platform branch
business_review.notes: sso.auth.ts:109 (chalk.white 'Opening browser…') then :112 (chalk.cyan fallback URL) — both appear before the if/else block, matching the spec verbatim

business_review.item: spawn options: { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' } and .unref()
business_review.notes: Implementation and test assertion both use 'detached' (correct Node.js API key). The spec doc had a minor 'detach' typo; implementation rightly uses the correct spelling.

business_review.item: No error handling on explorer.exe spawn — best-effort launch
business_review.notes: spawn call has no .on('error') handler. A deferred concern notes this could produce an unhandled EventEmitter crash if explorer.exe is missing, but the spec explicitly chose best-effort semantics and explorer.exe is always present on target Windows machines.

standards_review.notes: Commit message follows Conventional Commits (fix prefix with scope observed). Mixed-scope commit (SSO fix + statusline test fix) was caused by lint-staged running on all staged files simultaneously — not a workflow violation.
Printing the SSO URL is explicitly required by the acceptance criteria. The URL is a callback-port URL, not a token-carrying URL; no credentials are embedded.

standards_review.item: Security — URL passed to external process (explorer.exe)
standards_review.notes: URL scheme validation concern is pre-existing (the original open() call had the same exposure). Not a regression introduced by this diff. ensureApiBase() processes the URL before use.
